2605.03472.title: Detecting Stealth Sycophancy in Mental-Health Dialogue with Dynamic Emotional Signature Graphs
2605.03472.paper_summary: As conversational AI therapists are increasingly used in psychological support settings, reliable offline evaluation of therapeutic response quality remains an open problem. This paper studies multi-domain support-dialogue evaluation without relying on large language models as final judges. We use a direct LLM judge as a baseline that reads raw dialogue text and predicts whether the target response is harmful, productive, or neutral. We find that direct LLM judges and symmetric text-similarity metrics are poorly aligned with therapeutic quality because the target label depends on clinical direction: whether the response moves the user state toward regulation or reframing, leaves it broadly unchanged, or reinforces deterioration through higher risk affect or cognitive-distortion mass. To address this issue, we propose Dynamic Emotional Signature Graphs (DESG), a model-agnostic evaluator that represents dialogue windows with decoupled clinical states and scores them using asymmetric clinical geometry. We evaluate DESG on a constructed diagnostic stress-test benchmark of 3{,}000 dialogue windows from EmpatheticDialogues, ESConv, and CRADLE-Dialogue, covering peer support, counseling dialogue, and crisis-oriented interaction. On the 600-window held-out test aggregate, DESG-Ensemble achieves 0.9353 macro-F1, exceeding ConcatANN by 1.51 percentage points, BERTScore by 19.63 points, and TRACT by 33.81 points. Feature ablations, artifact controls, a 100-window blinded adjudicator audit, and qualitative disagreement cases indicate that the clinical state manifold is the main discriminative substrate, while graph-based trajectory components provide asymmetric scoring and interpretable diagnostics rather than serving as the sole source of performance.
2605.03472.paper_summary_zh: 隨著對話式人工智慧治療師在心理支持環境中的使用日益增加，可靠的離線評估治療反應質量仍然是一個未解決的問題。本文研究了多領域支持對話的評估，而不依賴大型語言模型作為最終判斷者。我們使用直接的LLM判斷者作為基準，該判斷者閱讀原始對話文本並預測目標反應是否有害、富有成效或中立。我們發現，直接的LLM判斷者和對稱的文本相似性指標與治療質量之間的對齊度較差，因為目標標籤取決於臨床方向：無論反應是否使用戶狀態朝向調節或重構，保持大體不變，還是通過更高風險的情感或認知扭曲質量來加強惡化。為了解決這個問題，我們提出了動態情感簽名圖（DESG），這是一種與模型無關的評估器，能夠用解耦的臨床狀態表示對話窗口，並使用不對稱的臨床幾何學對其進行打分。我們在構建的診斷壓力測試基準上評估DESG，該基準包含來自EmpatheticDialogues、ESConv和CRADLE-Dialogue的3,000個對話窗口，涵蓋了同儕支持、諮詢對話和危機導向互動。在600個窗口的保留測試總體中，DESG-Ensemble達到了0.9353的宏觀F1分數，超過了ConcatANN 1.51個百分點、BERTScore 19.63分和TRACT 33.81分。特徵消融、文物控制、100窗口盲審計和質性不一致案例表明，臨床狀態流形是主要的區分基礎，而基於圖的軌跡組件提供不對稱的打分和可解釋的診斷，而不是作為性能的唯一來源。
2605.03472.author: Tianze Han et.al.
2605.03472.authors: Tianze Han, Beining Xu, Hanbo Zhang, Yongming Lu
